name: Spinach-Cheese Puffs
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the spinach and squeeze dry with paper towels.
2. In a 3-quart saucepan over medium heat, heat milk, margarine or butter, and salt until the margarine melts and mixture boils.
3. Remove the saucepan from heat. With a wooden spoon, vigorously stir in flour all at once until the mixture forms a ball and leaves the side of the saucepan.
4. Add eggs to the flour mixture, 1 at a time, beating well after each addition until the mixture is smooth and satiny.
5. Stir in Gruyere and Parmesan cheeses and spinach. If not baking right away, cover the surface of the mixture with plastic wrap and refrigerate.
6. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
7. Lightly grease 2 large cookie sheets.
8. Drop batter by level tablespoons onto cookie sheets, about 1 1/2 inches apart.
9. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes until cheese puffs are golden brown. Arrange appetizer and garnish on platter; serve immediately.
10. MAKES 4 DOZEN CHEESE PUFFS.
